questions.text: The standard form of a quadratic equation is:
questions.explanation: A quadratic equation must have a squared term, so the coefficient of x² (a) cannot be zero.

questions.text: Which of the following is a quadratic equation?
questions.explanation: Expanding (x+1)² = 2(x-3) gives x²+2x+1 = 2x-6 => x²+7=0, which is quadratic. In D, x² cancels out.

questions.text: The roots of the quadratic equation x² - 0.04 = 0 are:
If 1/2 is a root of the equation x² + kx - 5/4 = 0, then the value of k is:
The discriminant of the quadratic equation ax² + bx + c = 0 is:
questions.explanation: The discriminant (D) is b² - 4ac, which determines the nature of roots.

questions.text: If the discriminant D > 0, the roots of the quadratic equation are:
questions.explanation: A positive discriminant implies two distinct (unequal) real roots.

questions.text: If the discriminant D = 0, the roots are:
questions.explanation: When D = 0, the quadratic formula gives x = -b/2a twice, meaning roots are coincident.

questions.text: If the discriminant D < 0, then the equation has:
questions.explanation: A negative value under the square root in the quadratic formula results in no real solutions.

questions.text: The roots of the equation 2x² - x - 6 = 0 are:
questions.explanation: By factorization: 2x² - 4x + 3x - 6 = 0 => 2x(x-2)+3(x-2)=0. Roots are 2, -3/2.

questions.text: The values of k for which the quadratic equation 2x² - kx + k = 0 has equal roots is:
The quadratic formula was famously popularized by which Indian mathematician?
questions.explanation: Sridharacharya derived the method of completing the square to solve quadratic equations.

questions.text: If the sum of the roots of the equation kx² + 2x + 3k = 0 is equal to their product, then k is:
Which of the following equations has 2 as a root?
The nature of the roots of 2x² - 4x + 3 = 0 is:
questions.explanation: D = (-4)² - 4(2)(3) = 16 - 24 = -8. Since D < 0, there are no real roots.

questions.text: The roots of the equation x² + 7x + 10 = 0 are:
questions.explanation: (x+2)(x+5) = 0 gives roots -2 and -5.

questions.text: If x=1 is a common root of ax² + ax + 3 = 0 and x² + x + b = 0, then ab is:
A quadratic equation can have at most how many roots?
questions.explanation: The number of roots of a polynomial equation is equal to its degree.

questions.text: The product of two consecutive positive integers is 306. The quadratic representation is:
The discriminant of 3x² - 2x + 1/3 = 0 is:
If the roots of px² + qx + 2 = 0 are reciprocal of each other, then:
questions.explanation: Product of roots = 1. c/a = 1 => 2/p = 1 => p = 2.

questions.text: Which of the following is the condition for real roots?
questions.explanation: Roots are real if the discriminant is either positive (distinct) or zero (equal).

questions.text: The roots of x² - 3x - m(m+3) = 0 are:
questions.explanation: x² - (m+3)x + mx - m(m+3) = 0 => (x-(m+3))(x+m) = 0. Roots are m+3, -m.

questions.text: A quadratic equation whose roots are 2 and 3 is:
If roots of x² + kx + 12 = 0 are in ratio 1:3, then k is:
questions.explanation: Let roots be m, 3m. m(3m)=12 => 3m²=12 => m=±2. Roots are (2,6) or (-2,-6). Sum = ±8 = -k.

questions.text: The value of √6+√6+√6... is:
questions.explanation: Let x = √6+x => x² - x - 6 = 0 => (x-3)(x+2)=0. x=3 (positive root).

questions.text: Sum of a number and its reciprocal is 2.5. The number is:
If the roots of ax² + bx + c = 0 are equal, then c is:
questions.explanation: b² - 4ac = 0 => b² = 4ac => c = b²/4a.

questions.text: The perimeter of a rectangle is 20cm and its area is 24cm². The side lengths are roots of:
questions.explanation: l+b = 10, lb = 24. x² - (sum)x + product = 0.

questions.text: Every quadratic equation can have at most one real root.
questions.explanation: It can have zero, one, or two real roots.

questions.text: The discriminant of x² + x + 1 = 0 is:
Roots of x² - 5x = 0 are:
If b² - 4ac is a perfect square, roots are:
questions.explanation: Square root of a perfect square is an integer, so the result is rational.

questions.text: Equation (x-2)² + 1 = 2x - 3 is:
Number of real roots of (x+1)² - x² = 0 is:
questions.explanation: x² + 2x + 1 - x² = 0 => 2x + 1 = 0. This is a linear equation with 1 root.

questions.text: A quadratic equation with rational coefficients has one root 2+√3. The other root is:
questions.explanation: Irrational roots occur in conjugate pairs for rational coefficients.

questions.text: Constant term of a quadratic equation is 0. One root must be:
questions.explanation: ax² + bx = 0 => x(ax + b) = 0. So x=0 is always a root.

questions.text: If a and c have opposite signs, the equation ax² + bx + c = 0 has:
questions.explanation: If a, c have opposite signs, -4ac is positive. b²-4ac is always positive, so roots are real.

questions.text: A quadratic equation with real coefficients can have one real and one imaginary root.
questions.explanation: Imaginary roots always occur in pairs for real coefficients.

questions.text: If the discriminant is 49, roots are:
questions.explanation: 49 > 0 and 49 is a perfect square.

questions.text: Standard form of x(2x+3) = 1 is:
questions.explanation: Multiply and move all terms to one side.
